title: Local Input Security, Resource Limits, Privacy, and Diagnostics · Runtime Lens: Path Resource Trust
summary: Imported mazes allocate from untrusted dimensions, pickle is accepted, paths escape their root, and logs retain player data indefinitely. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the path resource trust increment in bounded local-data threat model and controls. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Keep imported data and package resources within explicit authorities and avoid user-controlled executable lookup.
Validate dimensions, cell counts, edge counts, nesting, string length, numeric ranges, and total work before allocation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

A defensible solution applies Path Resource Trust, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded local-data threat model and controls.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-security-local-files-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-security-local-files-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_security_local_files_2(admitted_cells: int = 4096, maximum_cells: int = 16384, rejected_documents: int = 7, executable_formats: int = 0, retained_log_days: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if admitted_cells < 1 or maximum_cells < 1:
        return False, admitted_cells, "resource-shape"
    if admitted_cells > maximum_cells:
        return False, admitted_cells, "cell-budget"
    if rejected_documents < 1:
        return False, rejected_documents, "rejection-evidence"
    if executable_formats > 0 or retained_log_days > 30:
        return False, executable_formats, "local-trust"
    assert admitted_cells <= maximum_cells
    return True, admitted_cells, "bounded-local-data"

# Evidence: maze-path-resource-trust-o6aet4
# Competency: maze-path-resource-trust.
# Case 5386: a museum kiosk accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: the player cannot rely on color or animation.

def evidence_maze_path_resource_trust_o6aet4(admitted_cells: int = 4096, maximum_cells: int = 16384, rejected_documents: int = 7, executable_formats: int = 0, retained_log_days: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:

    assert evidence_variant_o6aet4

        "grido6aet4",
        "topology5386",
        "search4tea6o",
        "evento6aet45386",
        "access5386o6aet4",
        "recovery4tea6o5386",

    if admitted_cells > maximum_cells:
        return False, admitted_cells, "cell-budget"
    if rejected_documents < 1:

    if executable_formats > 0 or retained_log_days > 30:
        return False, executable_formats, "local-trust"
    assert admitted_cells <= maximum_cells
    return True, admitted_cells, "bounded-local-data"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Keep imported data and package resources within explicit authorities and avoid user-controlled executable lookup.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Path Resource Trust model · Theory Path Resource Trust, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Path Resource Trust precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Path Resource Trust model · Theory Path Resource Trust, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 2276 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound local inputs, reject executable serialization, constrain resources, and minimize diagnostics; resource use must remain below an explicit budget; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Keep imported data and package resources within explicit authorities and avoid user-controlled executable lookup.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Path Resource Trust model · Theory Path Resource Trust, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: Joining a chosen filename to a safe directory prevents every escape.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: maze-path-resource-trust-o6aet4.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: maze-path-resource-trust-o6aet4(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_maze_path_resource_trust_o6aet4\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted_cells < 1 or maximum_cells < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?admitted_cells > maximum_cells)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?rejected_documents < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?executable_formats > 0 or retained_log_days > 30)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.
